description:
You are acting as a data-scientist who has just received a very small transactions file that was generated on a machine set to the UTC time-zone and an unknown locale.  
Your objective is to (1) make sure your shell session is using the correct locale and time-zone variables, and (2) produce a “clean” version of the CSV whose date-time column has been converted from UTC to America/New_York.  
The automated grader will look for three artefacts, so follow the required filenames, formats and ordering exactly.

Initial state of the workspace
1. Directory /home/user/data already exists.
2. File /home/user/data/raw_transactions.csv already exists and contains exactly four rows (one header + three data rows) in UTF-8:
   id,product,price_usd,utc_time
   1,Book,12.99,2022-03-13T06:30:00Z
   2,Pen,1.50,2022-11-06T05:15:00Z
   3,Notebook,4.20,2022-12-01T16:00:00Z
3. Directory /home/user/task_logs is empty and writable by the current user.

Detailed requirements A-C below must all be satisfied.

A. Locale & time-zone log  
 • Within /home/user/task_logs create a plain-text file called locale_timezone_check.log  
 • The file MUST contain exactly two lines and nothing else.  
   Line 1  LC_ALL: en_US.UTF-8  
   Line 2  TZ: America/New_York  
 • Use a colon followed by a single space after each key as shown. No extra whitespace.

B. Create the cleaned CSV  
 • Create directory /home/user/data/cleaned (create parent directories as needed).  
 • Write the converted file to /home/user/data/cleaned/transactions_cleaned.csv  
 • The cleaned CSV must be UTF-8, contain a header row and exactly three data rows, comma-separated, **no quotation marks**, UNIX line endings.  
 • It must have these five columns, in this exact order and spelling:
     id,product,price_usd,utc_time,local_time  
 • local_time must represent the America/New_York wall-clock time in extended ISO-8601 with the numeric UTC offset (±hh:mm).  
 • Using correct DST rules the converted rows must look exactly like this (including offset values):

   1,Book,12.99,2022-03-13T06:30:00Z,2022-03-13T01:30:00-05:00  
   2,Pen,1.50,2022-11-06T05:15:00Z,2022-11-06T01:15:00-04:00  
   3,Notebook,4.20,2022-12-01T16:00:00Z,2022-12-01T11:00:00-05:00  

C. Cleaning steps log  
 • Create /home/user/task_logs/cleaning_steps.log  
 • Append (not overwrite) a short note for each high-level action you perform in chronological order, one action per line.  
 • The grader will only verify that at least three non-empty lines exist, so you may describe the steps in your own words.

Important rules & hints
• You do NOT have root; rely only on environment variables, local utilities (date, awk, python, csvkit, etc.) or small ad-hoc scripts.  
• Do NOT alter the original /home/user/data/raw_transactions.csv.  
• Make sure the numeric column price_usd retains the original decimal dot and has no thousands separator; any locale settings you apply must not change its formatting.  
• Anything you print to the console is fine, but the three deliverables listed above must exist exactly as specified for the automated grader to succeed.
truth:
Pre-existing file and its content:
/home/user/data/raw_transactions.csv   (4 lines total):
id,product,price_usd,utc_time
1,Book,12.99,2022-03-13T06:30:00Z
2,Pen,1.50,2022-11-06T05:15:00Z
3,Notebook,4.20,2022-12-01T16:00:00Z

Grader-expected artefacts after the agent finishes:

1. /home/user/task_logs/locale_timezone_check.log  (exact 2 lines, final newline optional):
LC_ALL: en_US.UTF-8
TZ: America/New_York

2. /home/user/data/cleaned/transactions_cleaned.csv  (exact 4 lines, final newline optional):
id,product,price_usd,utc_time,local_time
1,Book,12.99,2022-03-13T06:30:00Z,2022-03-13T01:30:00-05:00
2,Pen,1.50,2022-11-06T05:15:00Z,2022-11-06T01:15:00-04:00
3,Notebook,4.20,2022-12-01T16:00:00Z,2022-12-01T11:00:00-05:00

3. /home/user/task_logs/cleaning_steps.log  (>=3 non-empty lines, content not strictly checked).

The grader will fail if:
• Any file path differs, additional extensions appear, or directory names are misspelled.
• Whitespace deviates inside locale_timezone_check.log or inside the cleaned CSV.
• local_time values are missing, in the wrong time-zone, or lack the proper UTC offset.
